targetSubmarkets: West Valley (Glendale, Goodyear, Surprise, Buckeye growth nodes)
location.submarketGuidance: Projects in targetSubmarkets get a +1 score boost (per Tom's intake — these corridors matter more). Phoenix metro outside these areas still scores normally, just no boost.

differentiators: Coordination, constructability thinking, early cost clarity
New market entry — hungry for relationships and wins

strongFit: Mixed-use urban development (Edgewater Public Market, Littleton Mixed Use)
Hospitality — hotels, boutique restaurants, adaptive reuse (Courtyard Mile High, Catbird Hotel, AC Hotel RiNo, Warwick Hotel)
Institutional (education, healthcare, government — Founders Park, Harvard Square Memory Care)
Design-forward / boutique projects (Catbird Hotel, Jacques French, God Save the Cream)
Complex repositioning / additions / renovations (SW Denver YMCA, Warwick Hotel)
possibleFit: Senior living / memory care (proven: Harvard Square Memory Care)
concreteScopeOnly: ANY commercial project where a competitor GC is building
Structural concrete scope on projects Bristlecone didn't win as GC
Action: call the winning GC, not the developer
notAFit: Small projects under ~$2M (GC scope) or under ~$1M (concrete scope)
projectFit.bestClients: Private developers with repeat programs — large nationals or sharp boutique groups open to early collaboration. Especially where a project needs help getting 'unstuck' from cost or feasibility.

scoringGuidance.primaryTrack: Bristlecone's PRIMARY business is GC pursuit work — design-forward, technically demanding, ground-up or complex repositioning, negotiated/design-build/CM-at-risk preferred. This is the focus. Concrete sub-scope is a SECONDARY opportunity track, not co-equal. When in doubt, evaluate as a GC pursuit lead first.
scoringGuidance.actionability: Can Tom take a specific action? Parse his network for intros, make a phone call, send a LinkedIn message, submit quals? Score 9-10. 'The market is growing' with no specific action = score 3.
scoringGuidance.timing: Sites going under contract or pre-planning = 10. Just entitled = 9. Permitted and bidding = 8. Under construction = 5 (concrete scope still viable, GC can still change). Completed = 1. Tom says: years before a project starts is fine — never too early.
scoringGuidance.projectFit: Design-forward ground-up or complex adaptive reuse = 10. Distressed/foreclosed/stalled project needing rescue = 9 (see distressedSignals below). Small TI or single-family = 1. Concrete-scope-only on a large project where a competitor GC won = 6.
scoringGuidance.projectScale: Projects $10M+ for GC scope score highest. $2-10M solid. Under $2M filter out for GC. Concrete scope: $1M+ threshold.
scoringGuidance.competitivePosition: Project with no GC named = +1 boost. Project with GC but Tom knows someone on the team = neutral. Known target developer's project = +2 boost. Known target architect's project = +2 boost. Competitor GC won the job = concrete-scope lead, max score 6.
scoringGuidance.submarketBoost: If the project is in one of the targetSubmarkets (Downtown PHX, Camelback, Tempe Town Lake, Scottsdale Airpark, I-17 industrial, West Valley growth nodes), add +1 to the score. Outside Phoenix metro entirely (e.g. Tucson) = -1 unless otherwise strong.
scoringGuidance.gcFieldNote: GC field blank is a SIGNAL but does NOT dominate scoring. Tom: 'I don't care if there's a GC filled out or not.' Blank GC + Bristlecone sweet spot = strong GC pursuit lead. GC assigned and that GC is a competitor = concrete-sub lead (different action).
scoringGuidance.pursuitMatch: Tom's process: network intro first, then phone, LinkedIn, email, text, quals package. Action items should match this flow — e.g., 'Check your network for intros to Fields Development' before 'Send quals to owner.'
scoringGuidance.architectExtraction: ALWAYS extract architect names from project descriptions when present, and put them in key_contacts with role='Architect'. Tom doesn't have a target architect list yet, but every architect surfaced becomes a potential relationship. When an architect IS in targetArchitects, treat it as a warm lead and boost +2.

distressedSignals.description: Tom said 'broken, foreclosed, hairy projects' are right in Bristlecone's wheelhouse. Tom can't label these manually — Opus must detect them from text.
distressedSignals.scoringImpact: Any project with one or more distressedSignals keywords AND in Bristlecone's project type sweet spot = minimum score 7. Two or more keywords or explicit GC change = minimum score 8. Distressed projects are HIGH-VALUE leads — call them out clearly in the action_item with 'distressed/rescue opportunity' framing.

clientAdvocates: Tom has referral program active — names in his network. Tag referral leads with source.

emailSchedule.urgentAlerts: same-day for items scoring 9+ with time-sensitive deadlines (optional)

additionalRecipients: Phase 2 — CEO gets KPI view, precon gets bid dates

kpiMetrics.phase1: Raw leads surfaced → qualified leads → pipeline stages (weekly in email)
kpiMetrics.phase2: Full dashboard with estimated project start dates for cash flow forecasting
kpiMetrics.ceoNeeds: Data-oriented: lead volume, pipeline value, stage progression, win rate

fewShotExamples.title: Halo Vista Mixed-Use — N Phoenix (Empire Group)
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Empire Group is a target developer. Mixed-use is Bristlecone's sweet spot. Phoenix location. No GC assigned yet. fit_type=gc-scope (Bristlecone pursues this as GC). Tom should call his network for an intro to Empire Group's project lead immediately.

fewShotExamples.title: DPR Construction wins $80M data center — Chandler
fewShotExamples.reasoning: DPR (competitor) is EXPLICITLY NAMED as the winning GC, AND it's a data center (heavy structural concrete). Both conditions for concrete-scope are met. Action: call DPR's project exec to bid the structural concrete package. Capped at 6 because it's the secondary track. NOTE: this fit_type ONLY applies because DPR is named as winner. If no GC were named, this would be gc-scope.

fewShotExamples.title: Halo Vista mixed-use master plan announced — N Phoenix near TSMC
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Mixed-use master plan = Bristlecone sweet spot. Master plan stage means NO GC assigned yet → fit_type=gc-scope automatically (the rule is binary). N Phoenix near TSMC = +1 submarket/demand-driver boost. 'Near TSMC' does NOT make this a concrete sub job — TSMC is a neighbor, not the project. Score: base 8 (sweet spot, no GC, large scale) + 1 submarket boost = 9. Action: identify the developer immediately and pursue early preconstruction collaboration. This is exactly the kind of lead Tom would forward.

fewShotExamples.title: Opus Group breaks ground on innovation core — Peoria (West Valley)
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Opus Group is a major national developer with repeat programs (matches bestClients profile). 'Innovation core' suggests mixed-use or institutional. Peoria is West Valley = target submarket = +1 boost. No GC named. Score: base 7 + 1 submarket = 8. Action: research Opus Group's Phoenix lead via LinkedIn, leverage any second-degree connections.

fewShotExamples.title: Stalled Tempe boutique hotel back on the market after lender takeback
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Distressed signal (stalled, lender takeback) + hospitality + Tempe Town Lake area + Bristlecone's wheelhouse for hairy projects. fit_type=gc-scope. Action: research the lender or new owner immediately, lead with adaptive reuse + cost-rescue track record.

fewShotExamples.title: Construction employment up 26K nationally — wage pressure
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Industry stat with no project, no Arizona connection, no actionable contact. Drop. Should not have passed pre-filter.

fewShotExamples.title: Greystar 350-unit multifamily — Tempe Town Lake
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Greystar is a target developer. 350-unit multifamily is perfect Bristlecone sweet spot. Tempe is Phoenix metro. Pre-planning stage means Tom can pitch early preconstruction collaboration. Score 9 — act this week.

fewShotExamples.title: City of Scottsdale approves zoning for senior living facility — N Scottsdale
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Senior living is a possible fit. Just entitled means early timing (good). But no developer or GC named, no project scale. Action: monitor for developer announcement. Score 6 — watch list, not hot.

fewShotExamples.title: Phoenix metro construction activity report — Q2 2026
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Market intelligence report. No specific project, no actionable contact. Useful context for Tom's pipeline understanding but no immediate action. Score 4 — include in briefing for awareness.

fewShotExamples.title: New warehouse loading dock expansion — Buckeye
fewShotExamples.reasoning: Horizontal infrastructure / light industrial expansion. Not Bristlecone's fit type. No structural concrete scope likely. Buckeye is far west valley. Score 2 — drop.

minimumScores.pattern: A target developer (from targetDevelopers list) is mentioned as the owner/applicant for a Phoenix metro project
minimumScores.rationale: Tom asked for these — any target developer project is always a hot lead regardless of other factors

minimumScores.pattern: Multifamily 100+ units in Phoenix metro with no GC assigned
minimumScores.rationale: Bristlecone's core sweet spot with open GC opportunity

minimumScores.pattern: Project shows distressedSignals keywords AND fits Bristlecone's project types
minimumScores.rationale: Tom: broken/foreclosed/hairy projects are right in our wheelhouse

minimumScores.pattern: RFP with bid deadline in the next 30 days
Competitor GC wins a project $20M+ — concrete sub opportunity only
minimumScores.rationale: Concrete scope sub lead — different action (call the GC, not the developer). Capped at 6 because it's the secondary track.

minimumScores.pattern: Item is industry statistics, employment data, market roundup, or 'Top 5 deals' article with no specific named project
minimumScores.rationale: Not actionable — pure context. Should usually be filtered out before reaching evaluation.
